jobs.title: Patient Care Coordinator & Sales Consultant (Medical Spa)
jobs.description:
**About Nür Aesthetics Club**

Nür Aesthetics Club is a growing medical aesthetics practice in Santa Clara focused on providing exceptional patient experiences and natural\-looking results through injectables, laser treatments, skin rejuvenation, and wellness services.

We are seeking a highly motivated, sales\-driven Patient Care Coordinator who thrives on building relationships, converting leads, and helping patients achieve their aesthetic goals. This is not a traditional front desk position. We are looking for someone who treats clinic revenue, patient retention, and schedule optimization as their personal responsibility.

Position Overview

The Patient Care Coordinator serves as the first impression of Nür Aesthetics Club and plays a critical role in converting inquiries into booked appointments, maximizing provider schedules, and ensuring patients return for ongoing treatments.

The ideal candidate has experience in medical aesthetics, luxury hospitality, membership sales, or high\-end retail sales and is comfortable discussing treatment plans, pricing, memberships, and packages.

ResponsibilitiesSales \& Revenue Growth

* Convert inbound leads into consultations and treatments
* Respond to all phone calls, texts, website inquiries, and social media leads within 5 minutes whenever possible
* Present memberships, treatment packages, and skincare recommendations
* Track lead conversion metrics and continuously improve performance
* Follow up on all consultations and treatment recommendations
* Reactivate inactive patients through outbound calls, texts, and email campaigns
* Identify opportunities to increase patient lifetime value

Patient Experience

* Deliver exceptional customer service from first contact through follow\-up
* Build strong relationships with patients and maintain regular communication
* Manage patient scheduling to maximize provider utilization
* Ensure smooth check\-in and check\-out experiences
* Resolve patient concerns professionally and proactively

Operations

* Open clinic 10–15 minutes before the first patient
* Complete opening and closing procedures
* Maintain cleanliness and organization of the reception area
* Verify appointments and fill cancellations quickly
* Monitor daily schedules and implement same\-day fill strategies

Qualifications

* 2\+ years of sales experience preferred
* Medical spa, cosmetic, dermatology, dental, or luxury service experience strongly preferred
* Proven track record of meeting or exceeding sales goals
* Strong communication and relationship\-building skills
* Comfortable discussing pricing and closing sales
* Highly organized and detail\-oriented
* Experience with CRM or EMR systems preferred

Compensation

**Base Pay:** $30–$35/hour

**Performance Incentives:**

* Commission on memberships sold
* Commission on package sales
* Bonus opportunities tied to clinic revenue growth
* Health, dental, and vision benefits after successful completion of 90\-day introductory period

Success Metrics

A successful candidate will be expected to:

* Maintain lead response times under 5 minutes
* Achieve monthly membership sales goals
* Increase consultation\-to\-treatment conversion rates
* Improve schedule utilization and reduce gaps
* Reactivate inactive patients each month
* Contribute directly to monthly revenue growth

Pay: $25\.42 \- $30\.62 per hour

Work Location: In person

jobs.company: Lander Veterinary Clinic DBA Quality Milk Service
jobs.title: Large Animal Veterinary Receptionist & Client Care Coordinator
jobs.description:
**Large Animal Veterinary Receptionist \& Client Care Coordinator**

**Location:** Lander Veterinary Clinic, Inc. – Turlock, CA.

**Job Type:** Full\-Time

**About Us**

Lander Veterinary Clinic, Inc. is a progressive large animal veterinary practice serving livestock producers and ranchers throughout the region. In addition to comprehensive veterinary services, our clinic operates a full\-service pharmacy, diagnostic laboratory, embryo transfer division, and a small animal spay and neuter program. We are committed to delivering exceptional animal care, innovative reproductive services, and outstanding client support.

**Position Summary**

This position serves as the primary point of contact for clients and plays a critical role in scheduling farm calls, coordinating veterinarian appointments, supporting pharmacy operations, and assisting clients with products and services that support animal health and production goals. The ideal candidate is professional, dependable, detail\-oriented, and comfortable working in a fast\-paced agricultural environment.

**Key Responsibilities**

**Client Service \& Front Desk**

* Greet clients and visitors in a professional and welcoming manner.
* Answer and direct incoming phone calls.
* Assist clients with questions regarding appointments, services, and pharmacy products.
* Process payments, invoices, and client transactions.
* Maintain accurate client and patient records.

**Scheduling \& Coordination**

* Schedule and coordinate veterinary appointments, farm calls, herd visits, and emergency services.
* Communicate appointment details to veterinarians and support staff.
* Confirm appointments and follow up with clients as needed.
* Manage daily schedules to maximize efficiency and client service.
* Coordinate prescription refill requests and client notifications.

· Coordinate health certifications and facilitate compliance requirements for interstate transportation.

· Oversee scheduling, onboarding, and coordination of student externs and VFDs

**Pharmacy \& Inventory Support**

* Assist with prescription processing under veterinary supervision.
* Prepare medications and supplies for client pickup or delivery.
* Maintain inventory levels and assist with ordering products.
* Ensure accurate documentation and compliance with clinic protocols.
* Organize and stock pharmacy and retail areas.

**Sales \& Client Relations**

* Educate clients about veterinary services, herd health programs
* Promote clinic products and services that support animal health and productivity.
* Build strong relationships with producers, ranchers, and animal owners.
* Support marketing initiatives, seasonal promotions, and client outreach efforts.

**Qualifications**

* High school diploma or equivalent required.
* Previous experience in veterinary medicine, agriculture, animal health, medical reception, pharmacy, customer service, or sales preferred.
* Knowledge of livestock or agricultural operations is highly desirable.
* Strong communication and organizational skills.
* Ability to multitask and prioritize in a fast\-paced environment.
* Proficiency with computers, scheduling software, and basic office applications.
* Professional, dependable, and team\-oriented attitude.

**Preferred Experience**

* Large animal veterinary practice experience.
* Customer service and sales experience.
* pharmacy support experience.

**Physical Requirements**

* Ability to sit, stand, walk, and perform administrative duties throughout the workday.
* Ability to lift up to 30 pounds occasionally.

**Benefits**

* Paid time off and holiday pay (for eligible employees).
* Retirement plan and health benefits (if applicable).
* Opportunities for training

**Compensation**

**$25\.00\-$30\.00 per hour**, depending on experience and qualifications.

**Ideal Candidate**

We are looking for someone who enjoys working with agricultural producers and animal owners, excels at customer service, stays organized under pressure, and takes pride in supporting a veterinary team dedicated to the health and well\-being of livestock.

**To apply, please submit your resume and a brief summary of your experience in customer service, agriculture, animal health, or veterinary medicine**

Pay: $25\.00 \- $30\.00 per hour

Benefits:

* 401(k)
* Health insurance
* Life insurance
* Paid time off

Work Location: In person

jobs.title: RN Case Manager – Managed Care REMOTE
jobs.description:
**Job Title**: Registered Nurse (RN) Disease Manager

**Job Overview**:  

The Registered Nurse (RN) Disease Manager is responsible for coordinating care and managing the health outcomes of patients with chronic or complex health conditions. This role involves assessing, monitoring, educating, and supporting patients in managing their diseases to improve overall health and quality of life while reducing unnecessary hospitalizations and emergency care.

**Key Responsibilities**:

* **Patient Assessment**: Conduct comprehensive assessments of patients’ physical, emotional, and psychosocial needs related to their chronic conditions.
* **Care Coordination**: Work closely with interdisciplinary teams, including physicians, case managers, social workers, and other healthcare professionals, to develop and implement individualized care plans for patients.
* **Disease Education**: Provide education to patients and their families regarding their diagnoses, treatment plans, and self\-management techniques to improve disease outcomes.
* **Case Management**: Monitor patient progress and adjust care plans as necessary. Ensure that patients are following their prescribed treatment and managing their conditions effectively.
* **Data Management and Reporting**: Track patient outcomes and document interactions accurately. Utilize health information technology to document care, track patient progress, and report to appropriate stakeholders.
* **Advocacy**: Advocate for the patient’s needs, including obtaining necessary resources or services that will improve their health outcomes.
* **Prevention and Health Promotion**: Promote preventive care, including vaccinations, screenings, and lifestyle modifications to reduce the impact of chronic conditions.
* **Collaboration with Providers**: Maintain communication with healthcare providers to ensure continuity of care, timely follow\-ups, and updates on patient status.

**Qualifications**:

* **Education**: Bachelor’s Degree in Nursing (BSN) or an Associate's Degree in Nursing (ADN) with appropriate certifications.
* **Licensure**: Current Registered Nurse (RN) license in the state of employment.
* **Experience**: 3\-5 years of clinical nursing experience, preferably in disease management, case management, or a related field.
* **Certifications**: Certification in Disease Management (CDM), Case Management (CCM), or other relevant certifications preferred.
* **Skills**: Strong communication, problem\-solving, and critical thinking skills. Ability to work independently and as part of a team. Proficiency with electronic health records (EHR) and other healthcare technology.


\#Workwolf

jobs.title: RN Care Manager
jobs.description:
### **Role Summary**


Join a dedicated team where your work has a meaningful impact every day. As an RN Case Manager, you will play a vital role in supporting physicians and interdisciplinary teams to enhance patient care, improve clinical outcomes, and optimize healthcare resources. You will be responsible for coordinating care, facilitating transitions, and ensuring patients receive high\-quality, cost\-effective treatment.


This position offers flexible scheduling, opportunities for professional growth, and a supportive work environment committed to excellence in patient care.

### **Key Responsibilities**

* Manage a designated patient caseload, ensuring effective care planning and transitions.
* Facilitate precertification and authorization processes with payors.
* Collaborate with medical teams to coordinate patient care across the continuum.
* Identify and remove barriers to timely and efficient care delivery.
* Utilize process improvement methodologies to evaluate patient care outcomes.
* Ensure compliance with federal and state regulations, including CMS Conditions of Participation, EMTALA, and The Patient Bill of Rights.
* Perform admission, continued stay, and discharge reviews to determine medical necessity and resource utilization.
* Partner with healthcare providers and leverage evidence\-based practices to enhance patient outcomes.
* Other duties as assigned.

### **Required Qualifications**

* Associate’s Degree in Nursing (ADN) required.
* Current California RN license.
* 1\-3 years of hospital inpatient or related experience.
* Ability to effectively coordinate with provider networks, including Health Plans, IPAs, and FQHCs.

### **Preferred Qualifications**

* Bachelor of Science in Nursing (BSN).
* Certification in Case Management.
* Bilingual skills (Spanish preferred).
* Basic computer proficiency.
* Current Basic Life Support (BLS) certification.

### **Why Join Us?**

* **Competitive pay**: $53\.07 \- $82\.27 per hour.
* **Flexible scheduling** to support work\-life balance.
* Night shift employees receive a $7\.00 per hour differential.
* **Benefits**


	+ No cost medical, dental and vision option, Generous PTO plan, 403(b) employer match, Protective Equipment, Tuition reimbursement, Employee Wellness Program, Onsite childcare, Loan Forgiveness eligible facility, etc.

### **Work Environment**

* Full\-time positions available.
* 10\-hour daytime shifts


\#Workwolf

jobs.title: Patient Care Coordinator II - Primary Care Santa Clara
jobs.description:
1\.0 FTE Full time Day \- 08 Hour R2656912 Onsite 107097011 Primary Care Santa Clara Allied Health SANTA CLARA, 2518 Mission College Blvd, California
If you're ready to be part of our legacy of hope and innovation, we encourage you to take the first step and explore our current job openings. Your best is waiting to be discovered.

  


Day \- 08 Hour (United States of America)**This is a Stanford Health Care job.**  

  

**A Brief Overview**  

As a foundational level, the PCC II, is expected to learn and apply job skills, policies, and procedures to complete moderate assignments and tasks. They should exercise judgment within defined guidelines to determine appropriate actions. The PCC II responsible for facilitating smooth day\-to\-day clinic operations while developing the necessary skills to progress within our care delivery team.  

  

**Locations**  

Stanford Health Care  

  

**What you will do*** Key responsibilities include but are not limited to:
* Clinical Expertise Development: Undertake work in assigned areas to develop the necessary expertise within a given specialty area or clinic.
* Record Keeping and Protocol Compliance: Complete disability paperwork, maintain temperature logs, follow assigned protocols, and ensure workplace safety and infection prevention within the role's scope.
* Patient Care: Perform patient care duties, including measuring vital signs, weight, height, rooming, drawing labs, assisting providers with medication reconciliation, and other special departmental tasks such as EKG, ear lavage, etc.
* Clinical Administration: Handle check\-in/out, virtual rooming for providers, vaccine administration, point\-of\-care testing, and specimen collection.
* Clinic Maintenance: Manage weekly clinic preparation, clinic area stocking, medication, and supply ordering, and ensure timely completion of work assignments.
* Communication and Coordination: Handle patient phone calls, prescription refills, and clinic support operations, manage CRM/In\-Basket, and serve as a liaison between the provider, the patient, and the staff.

  

**Education Qualifications*** High School Diploma or GED equivalent AND one of the following: (1\) Certificate of completion or official transcript from a medical assisting program (2\) Completion of medical assistant training by a licensed physician or podiatrist (3\) Certification as medical assistant through a Medical Board of California – approved medical assistant certifying organization

  

**Experience Qualifications*** At least one year of overall experience (Including external experience) Required

  

**Preferred Knowledge, Skills and Abilities*** • Knowledge of and ability to use and apply medical terminology in performing his/her duties. • Knowledge of coding and billing regulations. • Knowledge of computer systems and software used in functional area. • Ability to speak and write effectively at a level appropriate for the job. • Ability to solve problems and identify solutions. • Ability to demonstrate customer service skills in interactions with all patients, families and staff, including high volume and stressful situations. • Ability to work independently as well as an integral part of the patient care team. • Ability to follow instructions and standard operating procedures. • Demonstrated familiarity, knowledge and understanding of home health, practice care coordination, clinical care skills. • Ability to demonstrate business communication skills including speak and write effectively at a level appropriate for the job (including legible penmanship/handwriting, knowledge of grammar and syntax and ability to write clear and concise clinical reports) • Ability to learn and grasp and update clinical knowledge on a regular basis (through HealthStream classes, continuing education and other work related courses and training) to adapt to an ever changing healthcare environment. • Ability to learn and understand population health and disease population concepts and practices; inventory methods and practices; motivation learning; customer relationships service concepts and practices

  

**Licenses and Certifications**
Current American Heart Association Certification for Basic Life Support for Healthcare Providers required  

* 

  

  

**These principles apply to ALL employees:**  

  

**SHC Commitment to Providing an Exceptional Patient \& Family Experience**  

  

*Stanford Health Care sets a high standard for delivering value and an exceptional experience for our patients and families. Candidates for employment and existing employees must adopt and execute C\-I\-CARE standards for all of patients, families and towards each other. C\-I\-CARE is the foundation of Stanford’s patient\-experience and represents a framework for patient\-centered interactions. Simply put, we do what it takes to enable and empower patients and families to focus on health, healing and recovery.*  

  

*You will do this by executing against our three experience pillars, from the patient and family’s perspective:** Know Me: Anticipate my needs and status to deliver effective care
* Show Me the Way: Guide and prompt my actions to arrive at better outcomes and better health
* Coordinate for Me: Own the complexity of my care through coordination

**Equal Opportunity Employer Stanford Health Care (SHC) strongly values diversity and is committed to equal opportunity and non\-discrimination in****all of****its policies and practices, including the area of employment. Accordingly, SHC does not discriminate against any person on the basis of race, color, sex, sexual orientation or gender identity and/or expression, religion, age, national or ethnic origin, political beliefs, marital status, medical condition, genetic information, veteran status, or disability, or the perception of any of the above. People of all genders, members of all racial and ethnic groups, people with disabilities, and veterans are encouraged to apply. Qualified applicants with crimin

jobs.title: Experienced Dental Front Office / Patient Care Coordinator (Part-Time)
jobs.description:
**Experienced Dental Front Office** (Part\-Time)

Established private family dental practice seeking a professional, personable, and highly organized Dental Front Office to join our team.

We are a respected family dental office with over 35 years of serving the community. Our practice is known for friendly patients, a supportive team environment, and high\-quality patient care. We are looking for someone who enjoys helping people, communicates well, and takes pride in keeping an office running smoothly and efficiently.

This position is ideal for someone with strong customer service and administrative skills who thrives in a detail\-oriented healthcare environment.

Schedule

Part\-time:

* 3\-4 days per week
* Additional coverage opportunities available from November 2026 through February 2027

Office Hours

* Monday: 8 AM – 4 PM
* Tuesday: 8 AM – 4 PM
* Wednesday: 9 AM – 2 PM
* Thursday: 8 AM – 4 PM
* Friday: 8 AM – 4 PM

Compensation \& Benefits

* $24–$36 per hour depending on experience
* Retirement plan
* Dental benefits
* Medical insurance
* Paid time off (PTO)

Ideal Candidate

We are looking for someone who is:

* Warm, polished, and professional with patients
* Calm under pressure and able to multitask
* Dependable, punctual, and team\-oriented
* Organized with strong attention to detail
* Comfortable learning dental and insurance systems
* Confident communicating by phone, text, and email
* Able to maintain professionalism in a busy office environment

Dental experience is preferred but not required for the right candidate with strong administrative and communication skills.

Responsibilities

* Welcome and check in patients
* Schedule and confirm appointments
* Answer phones, texts, and emails professionally
* Verify insurance eligibility and benefits
* Submit and track electronic insurance claims
* Collect patient payments and process transactions
* Coordinate with dental labs and referring offices
* Obtain patient records and X\-rays
* Help manage schedule openings and cancellations
* Follow up regarding overdue balances
* Prepare end\-of\-day reports and assist with monthly office accounting tasks
* Maintain a clean, organized, and professional front office

Preferred Experience

* Dental front office experience
* Medical or healthcare office experience
* Customer service or receptionist experience
* Familiarity with insurance verification
* Experience with scheduling software and basic office systems

Why Patients and Staff Love Our Office

* Long\-established, stable private practice
* Friendly patients and supportive team
* Positive and respectful work environment
* Opportunity to learn valuable dental office skills
* Consistent schedule with flexibility for additional hours

To Apply

Please send:

* Your resume
* A short introduction about yourself
* Your availability
* Relevant work experience

Pay: $24\.00 \- $36\.00 per hour

Benefits:

* Continuing education credits
* Employee discount
* Free parking
* Health insurance
* Health savings account
* Opportunities for advancement
* Paid sick time
* Paid time off
* Retirement plan

Work Location: In person

jobs.title: International Student Program Coordinator
jobs.description:
Crespi is currently seeking a full\-time International Student Program Coordinator.

Crespi’s mission is to build men of character and faith unified in brotherhood and formed through a contemporary, Catholic, college preparatory education in the Carmelite tradition of prayer, community, and service.

**Position Summary:**

The International Student Program Coordinator serves as the primary contact for international students and families, supporting them from admission through graduation, transfer, or departure. This role oversees onboarding, visa\-related documentation, regulatory compliance, student support, parent communication, and academic, cultural, and community integration. As part of the Admissions team, this position will work closely with Counseling, Registrar, Business Office, and Administration.

**Primary Job Duties:**

**International Admissions \& Enrollment Support**

* Serve as the school’s primary contact for prospective international students and families, as well as the Department of Catholic Schools Archdiocese of Los Angeles
* Coordinate with Admissions regarding inquiry follow\-up, application status, transcript review, English\-language readiness, grade\-level placement, etc.
* Review international student files for completeness before enrollment
* Coordinate collection, review, and secure storage of required documents (Passport, transcripts, financial documentation, guardian information, etc.)
* Coordinate acceptance communication with Admissions and Business Office to ensure that tuition, fees, deposits, and required financial documents align with I\-20 issuance
* Communicate to potential students the sequence of admission to the school, issuance of Form I\-20, payment of the required fees, Visa interviews, entry to the United States, etc.
* As the Coordinator,register, maintain, and update accurate SEVIS records for each F\-1 /M\-1 student
* Coordinate with the SEVIS Coordinator and SEVIS DSO regarding Form I\-17 updates, recertification, site visits, and school\-level compliance documentation
* Use SEVP and Study in the States guidance as the primary operational reference for F\-1 compliance

**International Student Onboarding \& Orientation**

* Design and implement a comprehensive onboarding process for new international students and families
* Coordinate orientation programs covering academic expectations, attendance requirements, school policies, technology access, student life, and campus resources
* Introduce students to the school’s culture, mission, traditions, and community expectation; partner with Counseling, Admissions, Academics, Dean of Students, Athletics, Campus Ministry, and Technology
* Promote international student participation in athletics, clubs, leadership opportunities, service programs, retreats, and campus life, fostering a sense of belonging and engagement within the school community
* Support cultural adjustment and help students navigate American educational and social norms

**Academic Monitoring \& Student Support**

* Monitor student academic progress, attendance, and overall adjustment throughout the school year
* Identify and support students experiencing academic, language, social, cultural, or behavioral challenges
* Coordinate interventions with Counseling for tutoring, academic support plans, teacher conferences, and parent meetings
* Help students understand graduation requirements, course sequencing, college planning, and academic integrity expectations

**Family, Guardian \& Agency Communication**

* Maintain regular communication with parents, guardians, host families, and educational agents
* Serve as the primary liaison for academic concerns, attendance issues, travel notifications, student wellness, discipline matters, and re\-enrollment discussions
* Maintain accurate records of key communications and ensure appropriate authorization for student\-related decisions
* Coordinate translation or language\-access support when needed

**Health, Safety \& Student Welfare**

* Maintain emergency contact, local guardian, and student residency information
* Coordinate with the Health Office regarding immunization compliance and health documentation
* Support students experiencing homesickness, anxiety, social isolation, family challenges, or other wellness concerns

**Faculty \& Staff Collaboration**

* Serve as a resource for faculty and staff regarding international student support
* Assist with training related to cultural adjustment, multilingual learners, family communication, and referral procedures
* Coordinate across departments to ensure a consistent and supportive experience for international students

**Supplemental duties may include:**

* Serve as a welcoming point of contact for prospective families by phone, email, and in\-person visits
* Support the planning and execution of admissions events
* Assist with year\-round campus tours
* Help maintain the annual admissions calendar
* Schedule student interviews with faculty and staff
* Assign or coordinate student ambassadors and tour guides
* Support FinalSite data entry, applicant tracking, and recruitment reporting
* Generate basic admissions reports as assigned
* Assist with application\-completion follow\-up
* Support accepted\-student onboarding and enrollment communication
* Help prepare admissions materials, signage, check\-in lists, name tags, folders, and family\-facing documents
* Collaborate with academic departments, athletics, advancement, and student services to support recruitment efforts

**Qualifications**

* Bachelor’s degree preferred; equivalent professional experience may be considered
* Experience in education, admissions, counseling, student services, registrar operations, or international student support
* Strong organizational, project\-management, and records\-management skills
* Excellent written, verbal, and interpersonal communication abilities
* Ability to travel internationally if needed
* Ability to work effectively with students a

jobs.title: Care Coordinator
jobs.description:
**Job Overview**  
We are seeking a dedicated and compassionate Care Coordinator (Certified Medical Assistant / Registered Medical Assistant) to join our healthcare team. This role focuses on Remote Patient Monitoring (RPM) and Chronic Care Management (CCM) in a direct to\-patient, outpatient setting. The ideal candidate has strong patient engagement skills, experience working with chronic conditions, and is comfortable supporting patients remotely while collaborating closely with providers and internal care teams. Duties \&

**Responsibilities** 

* Enroll eligible patients into Remote Patient Monitoring (RPM) and Chronic Care Management (CCM) programs, including patient education and consent documentation.
* Coordinate patient intake processes, ensuring accurate collection of medical history and completion of program\-related assessments.
* Educate patients on RPM devices, program expectations, and chronic condition management
* Monitor patient engagement, compliance, and vital sign trends through our platform and escalate concerns per protocol.
* Conduct routine and follow\-up outreach with patients to complete CCM check\-ins, care plan updates, and address care gaps.
* Document all patient interactions, assessments, and time spent accurately in the HealthArc platform to support program compliance and billing requirements.
* Collaborate with providers and internal care teams to support coordinated care within RPM and CCM workflows.
* Identify barriers to patient participation and implement strategies to improve adherence and engagement.

**Experience \& Qualifications** 

* Certified Medical Assistant (CMA) or Registered Medical Assistant (RMA) required.
* Minimum 4 years of experience as a Medical Assistant or Care Coordinator.
* Required: Hands\-on experience with Remote Patient Monitoring (RPM).
* Required: Hands\-on experience with Chronic Care Management (CCM).
* Experience using eClinicalWorks (eCW) EHR is a plus.
* Comfortable using Microsoft Teams for daily communication and collaboration.
* Strong patient engagement, communication, and documentation skills.
* Ability and willingness to work a Pacific Time Zone schedule.

Job Type: Full\-time

Pay: $19\.00 \- $21\.00 per hour

Application Question(s):

* Are you comfortable working in a PST shift?
* How many years of experience do you have with chronic care management?
* How many years of experience do you have with remote patient monitoring?

License/Certification:

* Certified Medical Assistant (Required)

Work Location: Remote

* Opportunities for training

**Compensation**

**$25\.00\-$30\.00 per hour**, depending on experience and qualifications.

**Ideal Candidate**

We are looking for someone who enjoys working with agricultural producers and animal owners, excels at customer service, stays organized under pressure, and takes pride in supporting a veterinary team dedicated to the health and well\-being of livestock.

**To apply, please submit your resume and a brief summary of your experience in customer service, agriculture, animal health, or veterinary medicine**

Pay: $25\.00 \- $30\.00 per hour

Benefits:

* 401(k)
* Health insurance
* Life insurance
* Paid time off

Work Location: In person

new_jobs.title: RN Case Manager – Managed Care REMOTE
new_jobs.description:
**Job Title**: Registered Nurse (RN) Disease Manager

**Job Overview**:  

The Registered Nurse (RN) Disease Manager is responsible for coordinating care and managing the health outcomes of patients with chronic or complex health conditions. This role involves assessing, monitoring, educating, and supporting patients in managing their diseases to improve overall health and quality of life while reducing unnecessary hospitalizations and emergency care.

**Key Responsibilities**:

* **Patient Assessment**: Conduct comprehensive assessments of patients’ physical, emotional, and psychosocial needs related to their chronic conditions.
* **Care Coordination**: Work closely with interdisciplinary teams, including physicians, case managers, social workers, and other healthcare professionals, to develop and implement individualized care plans for patients.
* **Disease Education**: Provide education to patients and their families regarding their diagnoses, treatment plans, and self\-management techniques to improve disease outcomes.
* **Case Management**: Monitor patient progress and adjust care plans as necessary. Ensure that patients are following their prescribed treatment and managing their conditions effectively.
* **Data Management and Reporting**: Track patient outcomes and document interactions accurately. Utilize health information technology to document care, track patient progress, and report to appropriate stakeholders.
* **Advocacy**: Advocate for the patient’s needs, including obtaining necessary resources or services that will improve their health outcomes.
* **Prevention and Health Promotion**: Promote preventive care, including vaccinations, screenings, and lifestyle modifications to reduce the impact of chronic conditions.
* **Collaboration with Providers**: Maintain communication with healthcare providers to ensure continuity of care, timely follow\-ups, and updates on patient status.

**Qualifications**:

* **Education**: Bachelor’s Degree in Nursing (BSN) or an Associate's Degree in Nursing (ADN) with appropriate certifications.
* **Licensure**: Current Registered Nurse (RN) license in the state of employment.
* **Experience**: 3\-5 years of clinical nursing experience, preferably in disease management, case management, or a related field.
* **Certifications**: Certification in Disease Management (CDM), Case Management (CCM), or other relevant certifications preferred.
* **Skills**: Strong communication, problem\-solving, and critical thinking skills. Ability to work independently and as part of a team. Proficiency with electronic health records (EHR) and other healthcare technology.


\#Workwolf

new_jobs.title: RN Care Manager
new_jobs.description:
### **Role Summary**


Join a dedicated team where your work has a meaningful impact every day. As an RN Case Manager, you will play a vital role in supporting physicians and interdisciplinary teams to enhance patient care, improve clinical outcomes, and optimize healthcare resources. You will be responsible for coordinating care, facilitating transitions, and ensuring patients receive high\-quality, cost\-effective treatment.


This position offers flexible scheduling, opportunities for professional growth, and a supportive work environment committed to excellence in patient care.

### **Key Responsibilities**

* Manage a designated patient caseload, ensuring effective care planning and transitions.
* Facilitate precertification and authorization processes with payors.
* Collaborate with medical teams to coordinate patient care across the continuum.
* Identify and remove barriers to timely and efficient care delivery.
* Utilize process improvement methodologies to evaluate patient care outcomes.
* Ensure compliance with federal and state regulations, including CMS Conditions of Participation, EMTALA, and The Patient Bill of Rights.
* Perform admission, continued stay, and discharge reviews to determine medical necessity and resource utilization.
* Partner with healthcare providers and leverage evidence\-based practices to enhance patient outcomes.
* Other duties as assigned.

### **Required Qualifications**

* Associate’s Degree in Nursing (ADN) required.
* Current California RN license.
* 1\-3 years of hospital inpatient or related experience.
* Ability to effectively coordinate with provider networks, including Health Plans, IPAs, and FQHCs.

### **Preferred Qualifications**

* Bachelor of Science in Nursing (BSN).
* Certification in Case Management.
* Bilingual skills (Spanish preferred).
* Basic computer proficiency.
* Current Basic Life Support (BLS) certification.

### **Why Join Us?**

* **Competitive pay**: $53\.07 \- $82\.27 per hour.
* **Flexible scheduling** to support work\-life balance.
* Night shift employees receive a $7\.00 per hour differential.
* **Benefits**


	+ No cost medical, dental and vision option, Generous PTO plan, 403(b) employer match, Protective Equipment, Tuition reimbursement, Employee Wellness Program, Onsite childcare, Loan Forgiveness eligible facility, etc.

### **Work Environment**

* Full\-time positions available.
* 10\-hour daytime shifts


\#Workwolf

new_jobs.title: Patient Care Coordinator II - Primary Care Santa Clara
new_jobs.description:
1\.0 FTE Full time Day \- 08 Hour R2656912 Onsite 107097011 Primary Care Santa Clara Allied Health SANTA CLARA, 2518 Mission College Blvd, California
If you're ready to be part of our legacy of hope and innovation, we encourage you to take the first step and explore our current job openings. Your best is waiting to be discovered.

  


Day \- 08 Hour (United States of America)**This is a Stanford Health Care job.**  

  

**A Brief Overview**  

As a foundational level, the PCC II, is expected to learn and apply job skills, policies, and procedures to complete moderate assignments and tasks. They should exercise judgment within defined guidelines to determine appropriate actions. The PCC II responsible for facilitating smooth day\-to\-day clinic operations while developing the necessary skills to progress within our care delivery team.  

  

**Locations**  

Stanford Health Care  

  

**What you will do*** Key responsibilities include but are not limited to:
* Clinical Expertise Development: Undertake work in assigned areas to develop the necessary expertise within a given specialty area or clinic.
* Record Keeping and Protocol Compliance: Complete disability paperwork, maintain temperature logs, follow assigned protocols, and ensure workplace safety and infection prevention within the role's scope.
* Patient Care: Perform patient care duties, including measuring vital signs, weight, height, rooming, drawing labs, assisting providers with medication reconciliation, and other special departmental tasks such as EKG, ear lavage, etc.
* Clinical Administration: Handle check\-in/out, virtual rooming for providers, vaccine administration, point\-of\-care testing, and specimen collection.
* Clinic Maintenance: Manage weekly clinic preparation, clinic area stocking, medication, and supply ordering, and ensure timely completion of work assignments.
* Communication and Coordination: Handle patient phone calls, prescription refills, and clinic support operations, manage CRM/In\-Basket, and serve as a liaison between the provider, the patient, and the staff.

  

**Education Qualifications*** High School Diploma or GED equivalent AND one of the following: (1\) Certificate of completion or official transcript from a medical assisting program (2\) Completion of medical assistant training by a licensed physician or podiatrist (3\) Certification as medical assistant through a Medical Board of California – approved medical assistant certifying organization

  

**Experience Qualifications*** At least one year of overall experience (Including external experience) Required

  

**Preferred Knowledge, Skills and Abilities*** • Knowledge of and ability to use and apply medical terminology in performing his/her duties. • Knowledge of coding and billing regulations. • Knowledge of computer systems and software used in functional area. • Ability to speak and write effectively at a level appropriate for the job. • Ability to solve problems and identify solutions. • Ability to demonstrate customer service skills in interactions with all patients, families and staff, including high volume and stressful situations. • Ability to work independently as well as an integral part of the patient care team. • Ability to follow instructions and standard operating procedures. • Demonstrated familiarity, knowledge and understanding of home health, practice care coordination, clinical care skills. • Ability to demonstrate business communication skills including speak and write effectively at a level appropriate for the job (including legible penmanship/handwriting, knowledge of grammar and syntax and ability to write clear and concise clinical reports) • Ability to learn and grasp and update clinical knowledge on a regular basis (through HealthStream classes, continuing education and other work related courses and training) to adapt to an ever changing healthcare environment. • Ability to learn and understand population health and disease population concepts and practices; inventory methods and practices; motivation learning; customer relationships service concepts and practices

  

**Licenses and Certifications**
Current American Heart Association Certification for Basic Life Support for Healthcare Providers required  

* 

  

  

**These principles apply to ALL employees:**  

  

**SHC Commitment to Providing an Exceptional Patient \& Family Experience**  

  

*Stanford Health Care sets a high standard for delivering value and an exceptional experience for our patients and families. Candidates for employment and existing employees must adopt and execute C\-I\-CARE standards for all of patients, families and towards each other. C\-I\-CARE is the foundation of Stanford’s patient\-experience and represents a framework for patient\-centered interactions. Simply put, we do what it takes to enable and empower patients and families to focus on health, healing and recovery.*  

  

*You will do this by executing against our three experience pillars, from the patient and family’s perspective:** Know Me: Anticipate my needs and status to deliver effective care
* Show Me the Way: Guide and prompt my actions to arrive at better outcomes and better health
* Coordinate for Me: Own the complexity of my care through coordination

Accordingly, SHC does not discriminate against any person on the basis of race, color, sex, sexual orientation or gender identity and/or expression, religion, age, national or ethnic origin, political beliefs, marital status, medical condition, genetic information, veteran status, or disability, or the perception of any of the above. People of all genders, members of all racial and ethnic groups, people with disabilities, and veterans are encouraged to apply. Qualified applicants with crimin

new_jobs.title: International Student Program Coordinator
new_jobs.description:
Crespi is currently seeking a full\-time International Student Program Coordinator.

Crespi’s mission is to build men of character and faith unified in brotherhood and formed through a contemporary, Catholic, college preparatory education in the Carmelite tradition of prayer, community, and service.

**Position Summary:**

The International Student Program Coordinator serves as the primary contact for international students and families, supporting them from admission through graduation, transfer, or departure. This role oversees onboarding, visa\-related documentation, regulatory compliance, student support, parent communication, and academic, cultural, and community integration. As part of the Admissions team, this position will work closely with Counseling, Registrar, Business Office, and Administration.

**Primary Job Duties:**

**International Admissions \& Enrollment Support**

* Serve as the school’s primary contact for prospective international students and families, as well as the Department of Catholic Schools Archdiocese of Los Angeles
* Coordinate with Admissions regarding inquiry follow\-up, application status, transcript review, English\-language readiness, grade\-level placement, etc.
* Review international student files for completeness before enrollment
* Coordinate collection, review, and secure storage of required documents (Passport, transcripts, financial documentation, guardian information, etc.)
* Coordinate acceptance communication with Admissions and Business Office to ensure that tuition, fees, deposits, and required financial documents align with I\-20 issuance
* Communicate to potential students the sequence of admission to the school, issuance of Form I\-20, payment of the required fees, Visa interviews, entry to the United States, etc.
* As the Coordinator,register, maintain, and update accurate SEVIS records for each F\-1 /M\-1 student
* Coordinate with the SEVIS Coordinator and SEVIS DSO regarding Form I\-17 updates, recertification, site visits, and school\-level compliance documentation
* Use SEVP and Study in the States guidance as the primary operational reference for F\-1 compliance

**International Student Onboarding \& Orientation**

* Design and implement a comprehensive onboarding process for new international students and families
* Coordinate orientation programs covering academic expectations, attendance requirements, school policies, technology access, student life, and campus resources
* Introduce students to the school’s culture, mission, traditions, and community expectation; partner with Counseling, Admissions, Academics, Dean of Students, Athletics, Campus Ministry, and Technology
* Promote international student participation in athletics, clubs, leadership opportunities, service programs, retreats, and campus life, fostering a sense of belonging and engagement within the school community
* Support cultural adjustment and help students navigate American educational and social norms

**Academic Monitoring \& Student Support**

* Monitor student academic progress, attendance, and overall adjustment throughout the school year
* Identify and support students experiencing academic, language, social, cultural, or behavioral challenges
* Coordinate interventions with Counseling for tutoring, academic support plans, teacher conferences, and parent meetings
* Help students understand graduation requirements, course sequencing, college planning, and academic integrity expectations

**Family, Guardian \& Agency Communication**

* Maintain regular communication with parents, guardians, host families, and educational agents
* Serve as the primary liaison for academic concerns, attendance issues, travel notifications, student wellness, discipline matters, and re\-enrollment discussions
* Maintain accurate records of key communications and ensure appropriate authorization for student\-related decisions
* Coordinate translation or language\-access support when needed

**Health, Safety \& Student Welfare**

* Maintain emergency contact, local guardian, and student residency information
* Coordinate with the Health Office regarding immunization compliance and health documentation
* Support students experiencing homesickness, anxiety, social isolation, family challenges, or other wellness concerns

**Faculty \& Staff Collaboration**

* Serve as a resource for faculty and staff regarding international student support
* Assist with training related to cultural adjustment, multilingual learners, family communication, and referral procedures
* Coordinate across departments to ensure a consistent and supportive experience for international students

**Supplemental duties may include:**

* Serve as a welcoming point of contact for prospective families by phone, email, and in\-person visits
* Support the planning and execution of admissions events
* Assist with year\-round campus tours
* Help maintain the annual admissions calendar
* Schedule student interviews with faculty and staff
* Assign or coordinate student ambassadors and tour guides
* Support FinalSite data entry, applicant tracking, and recruitment reporting
* Generate basic admissions reports as assigned
* Assist with application\-completion follow\-up
* Support accepted\-student onboarding and enrollment communication
* Help prepare admissions materials, signage, check\-in lists, name tags, folders, and family\-facing documents
* Collaborate with academic departments, athletics, advancement, and student services to support recruitment efforts

**Qualifications**

* Bachelor’s degree preferred; equivalent professional experience may be considered
* Experience in education, admissions, counseling, student services, registrar operations, or international student support
* Strong organizational, project\-management, and records\-management skills
* Excellent written, verbal, and interpersonal communication abilities
* Ability to travel internationally if needed
* Ability to work effectively with students a

new_jobs.title: Care Coordinator
new_jobs.description:
**Job Overview**  
We are seeking a dedicated and compassionate Care Coordinator (Certified Medical Assistant / Registered Medical Assistant) to join our healthcare team. This role focuses on Remote Patient Monitoring (RPM) and Chronic Care Management (CCM) in a direct to\-patient, outpatient setting. The ideal candidate has strong patient engagement skills, experience working with chronic conditions, and is comfortable supporting patients remotely while collaborating closely with providers and internal care teams. Duties \&

**Responsibilities** 

* Enroll eligible patients into Remote Patient Monitoring (RPM) and Chronic Care Management (CCM) programs, including patient education and consent documentation.
* Coordinate patient intake processes, ensuring accurate collection of medical history and completion of program\-related assessments.
* Educate patients on RPM devices, program expectations, and chronic condition management
* Monitor patient engagement, compliance, and vital sign trends through our platform and escalate concerns per protocol.
* Conduct routine and follow\-up outreach with patients to complete CCM check\-ins, care plan updates, and address care gaps.
* Document all patient interactions, assessments, and time spent accurately in the HealthArc platform to support program compliance and billing requirements.
* Collaborate with providers and internal care teams to support coordinated care within RPM and CCM workflows.
* Identify barriers to patient participation and implement strategies to improve adherence and engagement.

**Experience \& Qualifications** 

* Certified Medical Assistant (CMA) or Registered Medical Assistant (RMA) required.
* Minimum 4 years of experience as a Medical Assistant or Care Coordinator.
* Required: Hands\-on experience with Remote Patient Monitoring (RPM).
* Required: Hands\-on experience with Chronic Care Management (CCM).
* Experience using eClinicalWorks (eCW) EHR is a plus.
* Comfortable using Microsoft Teams for daily communication and collaboration.
* Strong patient engagement, communication, and documentation skills.
* Ability and willingness to work a Pacific Time Zone schedule.

Job Type: Full\-time

Pay: $19\.00 \- $21\.00 per hour

Application Question(s):

* Are you comfortable working in a PST shift?
* How many years of experience do you have with chronic care management?
* How many years of experience do you have with remote patient monitoring?

License/Certification:

* Certified Medical Assistant (Required)

Work Location: Remote
